message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abtsract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The emergence and rapid dissemination worldwide of a novel
                  <jats:italic>Coronavirus</jats:italic>
                  (SARS-CoV-2) results in decrease of swabs availability for clinical samples collection, as well as, reagents for RT-qPCR diagnostic kits considered a confirmatory test for COVID-19 infection. This scenario, showed the requirement of improve de diagnostic capacity, so the aim of this study were to verify the possibility of reducing the reaction volume of RT-qPCR and to test cotton swabs as alternative for sample collection. RT-qPCR volumes and RNA sample concentration were optimized without affecting the sensitivity of assays, using both probe-based and intercalation dyes methods. Although rayon swabs showed better performance, cotton swabs could be used as alternative type for clinical sample collection. COVID-19 laboratory diagnosis is important to isolate and restrict the dissemination of virus, so seek for alternatives to decrease the coast of assays improve the control of disease.
                </jats:p>
title: Resource optimization in COVID-19 diagnosis

reference.article-title: The Extraction and Recovery Efficiency of Pure DNA for Different Types of Swabs

reference.unstructured: CDC. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. Interim Guidelines for Collecting, Handling, and Testing Clinical Specimens for COVID-19. p. 1–5, 2020a. Available in: <https://www.cdc.gov/coronavirus/2019-ncov/lab/guidelines-clinical-specimens.html>.
